comments.body: @lsm5 @umohnani8 PTAL. I think I did this correctly.
Why is this going to 4.2 rather then 4.1?
Looks like tests are not happy?
I was under the assumption that we were bumping in lock step with podman. Are we supposed to be a release behind?

No Idea, I guess we never released for podman 4.1, So I am fine with the bump.  Just curious.
Matching podman versions, will probably make it easier for users to understand.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cdoern*, *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [cdoern,lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@cdoern LGTM, though I checked out your PR and don't see a git tag against the commit. If you're planning to do that post-merge, that's fine

> No Idea, I guess we never released for podman 4.1, So I am fine with the bump. Just curious. Matching podman versions, will probably make it easier for users to understand.

Agreed.

> @cdoern LGTM, though I checked out your PR and don't see a git tag against the commit. If you're planning to do that post-merge, that's fine

@cdoern that was my bad. Looks like `gh checkout #PR_NUM` doesn't fetch the tag. I added your fork as remote and then fetched the tag. LGTM

LGTM

Does the bot merge this or do we have to do it manually

> LGTM
> 
> Does the bot merge this or do we have to do it manually

@cdoern mentioned prior commits were manually merged, so I merged it just now.

On some other repos though I've seen the bot take its own time before doing the merge.
